title: Smoky Light Tofu Bhuna Stir-fry
description: A light and smoky Filipino dish featuring tofu as the main ingredient with a hint of midnight craving taste, served warm for an evening snack.

ingredients.name: Tofu
ingredients.notes: Ensure tofu is drained and pressed to reduce moisture.

instructions: Preheat your wok or a large skillet over medium-high heat. Add olive oil and swirl to coat.
Add sliced green peppers, onions, and garlic to the hot pan. Stir-fry for about 2 minutes until vegetables start to soften.
Chop tomatoes into small pieces and add them to the mix. Cook for another minute until they release their juices.
Mix together Tamarind Paste, Fish Sauce, salt, sesame seeds butter, and black pepper corns in a bowl. Pour this mixture over the vegetables.
Increase heat to high, then reduce back down to medium-high. Stir-fry for 3-5 minutes until sauce thickens slightly but still flows smoothly.
Add tofu cubes to the wok. Cover with a lid if using and stir-fry for another 5 minutes or until tofu is golden brown and heated through.
Turn off heat immediately after removing from the stove top, ensuring food stays warm without burning.
